name: ai_usage
summary: Workflow AI usage records keyed by workflow and instance, used for read-only AI token usage reporting.
safeReportingNotes: Use for read-only Workflow AI usage and token consumption reporting.
Join to workflow and runtime tables only after validating the target Workflow version.
warnings: Do not modify Workflow AI usage tracking records directly.
Token counts and provider values should be validated against Workflow application behavior before billing or operational reporting.

columns.name: workflow_id
columns.purpose: Workflow definition identifier associated with the AI usage event.

columns.name: instance_id
columns.purpose: Workflow instance identifier associated with the AI usage event.

columns.name: service_id
columns.purpose: Identifier for the AI service or provider used by the workflow activity.

columns.name: input_token_count
columns.purpose: Number of input tokens recorded for the AI usage event.

columns.name: output_token_count
columns.purpose: Number of output tokens recorded for the AI usage event.

columns.name: timestamp
columns.purpose: Timestamp when the AI usage event was recorded.

reportingExamples.title: Inspect representative rows
reportingExamples.note: Use TOP with a narrow column list when orienting yourself to table shape. Do not update rows.

reportingExamples.title: Recent records by timestamp
reportingExamples.note: Confirm the date column meaning before using it for operational reporting.
reportingExamples.sql:
SELECT TOP (100)
  [workflow_id],
  [instance_id],
  [service_id],
  [input_token_count],
  [output_token_count],
  [timestamp]
FROM [dbo].[ai_usage]
WHERE [timestamp] IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY [timestamp] DESC;
